title: Money Supply and Monetary Base
notes: Adds comparator lines (TUR, SAU, IRQ, VEN, ARG, RUS, USA, KOR; ESP and ITA have ZERO WDI observations for this indicator, confirmed via a live re-query, not a pull failure) using WDI FM.LBL.BMNY.GD.ZS (Broad money, % of GDP). This is a DIFFERENT UNIT from Iran's own chart (billion rials / real-2015-US$ levels) -- WDI does not publish comparable local-currency broad-money levels across countries of vastly different economy sizes, so % of GDP is the standard normalized proxy. Iran's own figure is kept as-is; comparators are added as a clearly separate, differently-labeled unit, not blended or adjudicated. Monetary base itself has NO comparator -- WDI does not publish a separate monetary-base series for most countries; only the M2/broad-money half of this chart gains comparators.

title: Government Debt to the Central Bank
notes: Adds comparator lines for all 10 roster countries using WDI FS.AST.CGOV.GD.ZS (Claims on central government, etc., % of GDP). IMPORTANT CAVEAT: this is IMF's broader monetary-survey concept -- claims on central government held by the WHOLE banking system (central bank + deposit money banks combined), NOT the central-bank-only claims that Iran's own chart measures. It is the closest available multi-country proxy, not an identical measurement; flagged here and in the processed-series README so it is never presented as an apples-to-apples match. Iran's own quarterly billion-rial levels are kept unchanged; comparators are % of GDP, a different unit, added as a separate series.

title: Annual Budget Law Totals
time_range: 1972-2024 (varies by country; VEN has zero observations for either series)
notes: Adds comparator revenue (GC.REV.XGRT.GD.ZS) and expenditure (GC.XPN.TOTL.GD.ZS, IMF GFS 'Expense') lines, both % of GDP, for TUR, SAU, IRQ, ARG, RUS, USA, KOR, ESP, ITA (VEN has zero WDI observations for either series -- confirmed, not a download gap). Iran's own chart is in nominal/real-2015-USD rial levels; comparators are added as a % of GDP, a different but standard normalized unit for cross-country budget comparison, kept as a separate labeled series alongside Iran's own figures.

title: Total Country Budget: Resources & Uses Breakdown (SH1385-1400)
notes: Same comparator series as the iranplanbudgetorg__annual_budget_law_totals row above (GC.REV.XGRT.GD.ZS / GC.XPN.TOTL.GD.ZS, % of GDP), attached separately to this SCI-sourced chart since it is a distinct, more granular measure (SCI's 'Total Country Budget' includes public-corporation/bank resources and uses on top of the core government general budget, which the WDI central-government GFS concept does NOT include). Comparators are most directly comparable to this chart's 'Government general budget resources' / 'Government general budget uses' variants specifically, not the broader 'Total country budget' variants that add public corporations -- noted here so a future editor doesn't read the comparator line against the wrong Iran variant.

title: Consumer Price Index, Urban vs Rural
notes: Adds a general (all-items, not urban/rural split) headline CPI inflation-rate comparator line, WDI FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG, for all 10 roster countries (coverage varies; ARG only 2018-2024, VEN only 2009-2016 -- both real WDI gaps). No source was found publishing an urban-vs-rural CPI split comparably across all 10 countries, so this is a general-inflation-rate comparator only, clearly not a matching urban/rural breakdown. Iran's own chart is an index level (1395=100); WDI's headline series used here is annual % change, a different but standard way to compare inflation trajectories across countries whose index base years differ (index levels with different base years are not directly comparable, so % change is the correct normalized substitute, not FP.CPI.TOTL's own 2010=100 index).

title: Consumer Price Index by COICOP Group (1395=100, SH1390-1399)
notes: Same WDI FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG general-inflation comparator as the urban/rural CPI row above, attached here too. Explicit caveat: no COICOP-group-level (food, housing, etc.) CPI breakdown was found published comparably across all 10 comparator countries in one consistent source -- this comparator line is all-items headline inflation only, shown for broad context, NOT a matching group-by-group breakdown. PPI comparators for the separate iran_cbi__ppi_general_and_sectors_1996_2023 chart were investigated and found UNREACHABLE this pass (IMF/OECD API attempts failed or returned no usable dataflow; WDI's wholesale-price-index proxy FP.WPI.TOTL was rejected as too sparse/stale -- missing entirely for USA/RUS/ARG, stops in the 1990s-2000s for most others) -- no staging row proposed for that chart; see logs/downloads/comparator-monetary-prices.log for the full attempt trail.

title: Private Credit by Deposit Money Banks to GDP (World Bank GFDD)
notes: Adds comparator lines for all 10 roster countries, same GFDD.DI.01 indicator as Iran's own chart -- a genuine same-database match, not a proxy. Pulled directly via api.worldbank.org/v2 (new raw data, see data/raw/worldbank-gfdd/comparators-monetary-prices-2026-07-14/), since the existing Iran-only pull used the FRED mirror with Iran-specific series IDs. All 10 comparators have data.

title: Deposit Money Bank Assets to Deposit Money Bank Assets and Central Bank Assets (World Bank GFDD)
time_range: 1960-2021 (varies by country; SAU has zero observations)
notes: Same GFDD.DI.04 indicator as Iran's chart, pulled fresh for all 10 roster countries. SAU has ZERO observations for this indicator in GFDD -- confirmed via live query, a genuine source gap, not a request failure. All other 9 comparators have data.

title: Bank Deposits to GDP (World Bank GFDD)
notes: Same GFDD.OI.02 indicator as Iran's chart, pulled fresh for all 10 roster countries. All 10 comparators have data.

title: Bank Credit to Bank Deposits (World Bank GFDD)
notes: Same GFDD.SI.04 indicator as Iran's chart, pulled fresh for all 10 roster countries. All 10 comparators have data.

title: Iranian Insurance Market: Earned Premium, Incurred Loss & Loss Ratio
time_range: 1990-2020 (varies by country; IRQ has zero observations)
notes: Adds life (GFDD.DI.09) and non-life (GFDD.DI.10) insurance premium volume, % of GDP, comparator lines for TUR, SAU, VEN, ARG, RUS, USA, KOR, ESP, ITA (IRQ has ZERO GFDD insurance-sector observations at all, confirmed live -- Iraq gets no comparator line here). New raw data pulled directly from World Bank GFDD via api.worldbank.org, not previously in this project (see data/raw/worldbank-gfdd/comparators-monetary-prices-2026-07-14/). Different unit from Iran's own chart (million IRR level, whole-market total across all classes) -- comparators are % of GDP, a standard normalized cross-country measure, kept as a clearly separate series, not blended with Iran's own figure. Iran itself also has GFDD DI.09/DI.10 coverage (1991-2018) included in the same processed file for a direct-comparison sanity check, but Iran's Bimeh Markazi Iran own-sourced level series remains the chart's primary Iran figure, unchanged.

title: Iranian Insurance Market: Direct Premium Written & Growth Rate
time_range: 1990-2020 (varies by country; IRQ has zero observations)
notes: Same GFDD.DI.09 (life) / GFDD.DI.10 (non-life) insurance-premium-to-GDP comparator series as the earned-premium chart above, attached separately to this direct-premium-written chart since it is a distinct Iran chart_id (shorter window, 1379-1384/2000-2005, gross direct premium rather than earned premium). Same IRQ gap and same different-unit caveat apply.
